cells.value: Market-sizing of the outsourced new-construction SUPPLIER opportunity for US Navy submarines
(Virginia LI 2013 + Columbia LI 1045), for market entrant Saronic.
TAM is built from two cost streams (Basic Construction + AP/LLTM); SAM is a scenario menu.
Every registered/headline deck figure ties to a workbook cell via Deck_Outputs; appendix contrast figures live in the evidence tabs (not all registered). Companion: DDG-51 workbook.
Audit-color key (font color = edit-state; numeric cells only; NO yellow fills)
Text labels stay black regardless of edit-state
TAM = BC_base x headline_coeff (~35.0%, applied: non-nuclear, BPMI excluded)  +  AP/LLTM_base x AP/LLTM_coeff (~48.5%)
Coefficients are $-weighted supplier+foreign POP shares (POP_Coefficients); all-gated POP anchor = 51.8% (drift guard, not applied).
GFE + naval-nuclear LLTM (BPMI) dropped from BOTH base and corpus (nuclear-boundary base<->corpus fix).
AP/LLTM additive base = 0 (CONFIRMED): the supplier LLTM is already inside Basic Construction (no double-count).
SAM = SUMPRODUCT(bucket_$, scenario_flags) - a leadership menu, no fit/SOM haircut.
Coefficient legend: 35.0% = headline (applied, non-nuclear) · 75.7% = pre-nuclear-boundary sensitivity (NOT applied) · 51.8% = all-gated anchor (drift guard).
BC-only TAM x single ~52% coefficient; SAM x capability_fit x addressability
Two-stream TAM (BC + AP/LLTM, own coefficients); SAM = scenario menu (no haircut)
GFE excluded from BC corpus -> BC 61% to 75.7%; MIB column dropped (subaward-only)
Phase 3: BPMI naval-nuclear out of BC corpus (base<->corpus fix) -> BC 75.7% to 35.0%, TAM $42.9B to ~$20B; AP/LLTM base = 0 confirmed (supplier LLTM in BC)
Consolidation: 27 sheet modules -> 8 source files; 27 tabs -> 10 navigational tabs (rollup on top, full evidence below in collapsible groups). All figures + the audit chain preserved.
scope + two-stream at-a-glance + Market_Definitions, Exclusion_Rules, Bucket_Taxonomy, Worktype_Rules (this tab)
SCN P-5c per-FY detail + LLTM P-10 bucket grid + AP->TAM reconciliation bridge
Entity_Master vendor classification + Location_Master geography + Worktype_Evidence
Budget_Normalized stream bases + POP_Coefficients + two-stream TAM + annualization
Target_Scenarios matrix + Subaward bucket shares + TAM_Bucket_Allocation + scenario SAM
one cross-sheet link per deck figure (cumulative + average-annual)
MIB Excluded + Sensitivity + Number_Audit tie-out + QA_Reconciliation gate
TAM - the non-GFE, non-MIB new-construction supplier opportunity funded through Basic
Construction + AP/LLTM/EOQ (covered vessels), performed AWAY from prime / co-prime /
prime-controlled / GFE-controlled sites - the dollars genuinely available to outside suppliers.
SAM - the portion of TAM in work-type buckets Saronic may choose to target, shown as a
scenario menu (no capability-fit / addressability / win-probability haircut).
Distributed-production view (appendix) - work away from the final assembly yard (incl. co-prime);
broader than the commercial TAM, shown for context only.
P-5c BC + P-10 AP/LLTM/EOQ, net exclusions
BC_base x BC_coeff + AP_base x AP_coeff
TAM x modeled bucket share per (program, stream)
Sum of bucket $ where scenario flag = 1
Two-stream TAM (v5): TAM = BC_base x BC_coeff + AP/LLTM_base x AP/LLTM_coeff
supplier+foreign over non-GFE construction + component procurement
supplier+foreign over long-lead material / advance procurement
(GFE excluded from both base and corpus - strict v5; for submarines the AP/LLTM additive base = 0, confirmed - supplier LLTM is already inside Basic Construction)
Allocate TAM into 7 work-type buckets (by program x stream), then select buckets per scenario:
SAM_scenario[k] = SUMPRODUCT(bucket_$, scenario_flag[k, bucket])   with flag in {0,1}
No capability_fit, no addressability, no SOM-style haircut - those are win-probability, not SAM.
"If leadership targets these buckets, the opportunity is $X."
Governing principle - the award-action SCOPE controls (entity/location are hints only)
An award is EXCLUDED from TAM when its scope is GFE / weapons / sensors / ordnance /
Navy-directed systems procurement, MIB / industrial-base capacity, sustainment, depot, or
design-only - even if the work physically supports or installs on a covered vessel.
scope_class vocabulary (POP_Location_Parse) - sub uses the lean subset
long-lead material / early mfg / advance proc
combat systems, sonar, weapons, ordnance, sensors
dropped from base + coefficient corpus (strict v5)
BlueForge/TMG/IALR; not in the gated POP corpus
hull sections, fabricated structural metal, pre-outfit modules
machine shops, precision machining, mechanical power transmission
iron/steel forging, steel foundries, cast components
industrial valves, pumps, measuring/dispensing, pipe & fittings
parent-unknown, naval-nuclear specialty, or no clean NAICS
2. GFE / MIB (combat systems, sonar, BlueForge) -> excluded (role=gfe_mib)
3. vendor-name override -> bucket (5511 holding cos, 3364 propulsion-elec, blank-NAICS names)
4. NAICS-4digit crosswalk -> role=supplier, bucket per NAICS
6. residual -> role=supplier, bucket=unbucketed (shown, never silently spread)
Arbiter principle - subawards classify the buckets (not the total-mix truth)
Subawards map vendor / description / NAICS -> bucket and SEED the observed shares,
but the visible subaward mix is never assumed to equal the hidden TAM mix - the modeled
bucket share is adjusted transparently (Subaward_Clean_Bucketed), residual shown explicitly.
Submarine = NAICS / vendor-led ; DDG = description-led. Entity/location are hints only.
Lockheed combat sys, Ultra sonar, BlueForge
Override artifacts (name-based fixes for NAICS that mislead)
NG / Leonardo coded as aircraft mfg
-> vendor-name override to the right bucket
specialty reactor work, not a clean bucket
